steps.title: Identify the grammatical role needed
steps.content: The blank is the subject of the verb phrase “are descendants of Proto-Kru” inside the subordinate clause “because ______ are descendants of Proto-Kru.” The blank needs a plural subject pronoun referring to “thirty-eight languages.”

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘its’: Singular possessive pronoun. Violates two rules: wrong case (possessive, not subject) and wrong number (singular vs. plural “languages”). ✗
  • Choice B — ‘they’re’: Contraction of “they are” — plural subject pronoun “they” followed by “are,” which agrees with plural “languages.” Correctly fills the subject slot. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘their’: Plural possessive pronoun. Violates pronoun case — a possessive cannot serve as the subject of “are descendants.” ✗
  • Choice D — ‘it’s’: Contraction of “it is” — singular subject. Violates subject-verb agreement with plural “thirty-eight languages.” ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
